data.narrative:
The Nuclear Company is a Series A frontier-tech venture in the nuclear energy sector, having raised $50M+ to date. The valuation landscape includes 3 public comparables (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private peers, including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, and Kairos Power, though narrow-filter peer matching yielded insufficient data for reliable median multiples.

Stage-progression methodology is the primary applicable approach. Using total raised ($50M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical for Series A-to-next-stage progression yields $100M. This single-method triangulation establishes a reasonable central estimate. Peer-median comparison was not feasible; while 8 same-sector private peers exist, applying conventional narrow filters (same stage, adjacent valuation tier) produced fewer than the 3 datapoints needed for robust statistical grounding. Broader sector median comparison risks false precision given the heterogeneity within nuclear subsectors.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $85M–$115M, with $100M as the median point. Key uncertainties include the representative adequacy of the 2.0x multiplier for early-stage nuclear ventures (which may command different step-ups than broader venture cohorts), the absence of same-stage peer multiples to anchor relative value, and near-term technical or regulatory risk specific to The Nuclear Company's approach. Additional data on peer funding rounds, burn rates, and technology milestones would strengthen confidence in this range.
